CONCEPTION AND BIRTH 121
why our ancients prescribed only certain days and prohibited
the rest for sexual congress. When modem sciences are taking
long strides, these rules are considered by many as a bundle of
superstitions worthy of being discarded. If Mars and Saturn
should be opposite, i.e. in the 7th house, from the Sun at the time
of copulation, the husband would fall ill. Similarly if these
planets should be in the 7th house from the Moon, the wife
would suffer. Death would be the result if the Sun or the Moon,
as the case may be, should have Mars and Saturn in the J2th
and 2nd places, or either of the luminaries is aspected by one of
these malefics and conjoined with the other. It has been already
pointed out to you that for a day-birth the Sun and Venus repre-
sent the father and mother respectively and Saturn and the Moon
the paternal uncle and maternal aunt respectively. At night this
order is to be reversed.
